title: Stable Merge Sort with Missing Swap

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, implement a stable merge sort algorithm that sorts the array in ascending order. The merge function is almost complete but is missing a crucial swap operation. Identify and correct this bug.
statement.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer n, indicating the size of the array. The second line contains n space-separated integers representing the elements of the array.
statement.outputFormat: The sorted array in ascending order.
statement.notes: The merge function should be implemented using recursion. The merge function should create temporary arrays for merging two subarrays. The merge function should correctly handle the swap operation to maintain the stability of the merge sort algorithm.

examples.explanation: The input array {1, 4, 2, 3, 5} is sorted in ascending order.

hints.text: The missing swap operation in the merge function is crucial for maintaining the stability of the merge sort algorithm.

editorial.title: Stable Merge Sort with Missing Swap Editorial
editorial.intuition: The merge function should correctly handle the swap operation to maintain the stability of the merge sort algorithm.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to correctly implement the swap operation in the merge function to maintain the stability of the merge sort algorithm.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is based on the fact that the merge function correctly handles the swap operation, ensuring that the merge sort algorithm maintains stability.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run with correct implementation of the swap operation
commonMistakes: Missing swap operation in the merge function

functionSignature.cpp: void merge(vector<int>& arr, int left, int mid, int right);
functionSignature.java: void merge(int[] arr, int left, int mid, int right);
functionSignature.python: def merge(self, arr, left, mid, right):
